learning_outcomes.description: Explain the nature of scientific data and the need for statistical analysis.
Construct probabilistic models of random phenomena and derive quantities of interest including expectation and variance.
Explain the behaviour of hypothesis tests under null and alternative hypotheses, including Type I error rates, power, and the interpretation of p-values.
Identify and critically evaluate ethical issues in scientific research.
Demonstrate a foundational knowledge of statistical methods by being able to carry out simple statistical procedures by hand.
Use statistical software appropriately and confidently for exploratory data analysis and to make relevant statistical conclusions.
Plan and carry out a research project, and analyse and communicate the results.

assessments.due_date: Applied class exercises will be completed each week from Week 2 to Week 12, except in Week 5, when the in-class quiz will be held instead.
assessments.task_description:
You must attend your applied classes in person. In each class, you will receive a task sheet containing activities designed to support your learning, including questions modelled on the style of STAT1301 exam questions. Some tasks may also require you to write R code.

Each applied class exercise will be marked out of 1. You may work in groups on the provided task. To earn the point, you must arrive on time, remain engaged with the task throughout the session, and contribute to the written solutions.

Your mark for this component will be calculated from your best eight of ten applied class exercises. This means you may miss two applied classes without penalty.

Applied class scores will be uploaded to Blackboard the week after the class runs. It is your responsibility to check these marks and follow the procedure outlined on Blackboard if you have any concerns or think that a mark has been missed.
assessments.submission_guidelines: The task must be completed during the applied class and submitted in person at the end of the class.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.late_submission: You will receive a mark of 0 if this assessment is submitted late.
assessments.additional_info: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

assessments.task_description: An in-class quiz will take place in Week 5. The quiz will be completed in class and will consist of 60 minutes of working time and 10 minutes of preparation time.
assessments.submission_guidelines: The quiz must be completed and submitted in person during the usual Week 5 lecture.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.

assessments.task_description: As part of the Research Project you will study a population of virtual subjects to collect data, and then analyse the results and present them in the form of a condensed scientific report.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
The End of Semester examination in this course will be held during the end-of-semester examination period. It will be an in-person exam held on campus. 

The two-hour examination will cover all the statistical and ethics material in the course. The exam paper will come with a formula sheet and statistical tables.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.
